data.description:
## Context / Problem
An LLM plan review can consume the full model cost, return `PASS`, and still leave the reviewed ticket without a signed plan-review attestation unless signing was separately requested. That discards the review's durable gate artifact: the subsequent claim path cannot distinguish the paid, passing review from no review at all and may require another billable run or a manual recovery step.

The intended contract is already stated by the dedicated `rebar review-plan` documentation and library docstring: a non-blocking PASS signs the plan-review attestation consumed by the claim gate. The observed behavior shows that at least one public plan-review route exposed through `rebar review`/plan dispatch does not preserve that contract. All public routes that perform the same billable plan review must converge on one default: sign a fresh attestation after PASS. Deliberately skipping the signature must be explicit, visible, and exceptional.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Create or select an open, non-exempt ticket with a reviewable plan and no current plan-review attestation.
2. Run the plan-review operation through the affected `rebar review` plan route without an explicit no-sign option.
3. Allow the LLM review to complete with verdict `PASS`.
4. Run `rebar verify-signature <ticket-id> --output json`.
5. Observe that verification reports the ticket as unsigned even though the full review cost was incurred and no opt-out was requested.

## Expected Behavior
Every mutating public plan-review entry point signs and persists a plan-review attestation by default when the final verdict is a signable, non-degraded `PASS`. The attestation binds the reviewed material fingerprint and verified source SHA exactly as the claim gate expects.

Skipping signing requires an explicit opt-out at the relevant boundary: `--no-sign` for CLI use, `sign=False` for library/MCP use, or an explicitly configured read-only execution mode that is surfaced in the result. The output clearly states whether the attestation was signed and, when deliberately skipped, why.

## Actual Behavior
At least one `rebar review` plan path completes a billable PASS without signing by default. The user must notice the missing attestation and separately arrange signing or repeat work. This conflicts with the documented `review-plan` contract and makes behavior depend on which public route invoked the same logical operation.

## Approach
1. Inventory the CLI dispatcher, dedicated `review-plan` command, library `review_plan`, and MCP `review_plan` call paths and identify where the default changes or the signing wrapper is bypassed.
2. Route every mutating plan-review surface through the same sign-on-PASS finalization seam. Keep the structural guards that prohibit signatures for BLOCK, INDETERMINATE, degraded, local/unattested-source, and otherwise non-signable results.
3. Make deliberate unsigned execution explicit at each public boundary. CLI exposes `--no-sign`; library and MCP expose `sign=False`; read-only MCP mode remains unsigned but must return a machine-readable reason identifying the read-only policy.
4. Preserve idempotent reuse of an already-current attestation. A default invocation should neither rerun the LLM nor emit a duplicate signature when the claim gate already accepts the current attestation.
5. Preserve signing-failure behavior: a PASS whose signature persistence was attempted but failed is not reported as ordinary success. It returns the established retryable failure disposition and points to the non-LLM `sign-review` recovery path when the PASS sidecar remains current.
6. Update user, CLI, MCP, and LLM-framework documentation so the default, opt-out syntax, read-only exception, non-signable verdicts, and recovery behavior match the implementation.

Rejected: leaving signing opt-in to avoid ticket writes. The review is already a gate operation whose purpose is to produce the claim-consumable attestation; callers needing a pure read have explicit no-sign/read-only mechanisms. Rejected: signing BLOCK, INDETERMINATE, degraded, or local-source results, because those are not certifiable PASS outcomes.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py`
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py`
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`
- `src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py`
- plan-review dispatcher/finalization modules found during the call-path inventory
- `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py`
- `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py`
- MCP/library surface tests for plan review
- `docs/cli-reference.md`
- `docs/user-guide.md`
- `docs/llm-framework.md`
- `docs/plan-review-gate.md`
- `docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md`
- generated MCP reference inputs/output if the MCP signature changes

## Testing / Verification
- Unit tests cover sign-on-PASS and never-sign verdict/source guards at the shared finalization seam.
- CLI tests exercise default signing and explicit `--no-sign` through the actual argument parser and dispatcher.
- Library and MCP contract tests exercise default `sign=True`, explicit `sign=False`, and read-only behavior.
- An end-to-end lifecycle test runs a passing plan review through the affected public route, verifies the resulting signature, and then proves the claim gate accepts it without a second LLM call.
- Documentation/help drift checks pin the default and every supported opt-out/recovery path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A non-exempt ticket receiving a non-degraded PASS through each mutating public plan-review entry point gets a persisted plan-review attestation by default; interface tests verify `rebar verify-signature <ticket-id> --output json` returns `verified=true`.
- [ ] An end-to-end lifecycle test invokes the affected `rebar review` plan route without an opt-out, verifies the attestation matches the current material fingerprint, and proves a subsequent `rebar claim` succeeds without another LLM call.
- [ ] CLI unsigned execution requires the explicit `--no-sign` flag; parser tests prove omission signs and presence deliberately skips signing.
- [ ] Library and MCP unsigned execution requires explicit `sign=False`, except for explicitly configured MCP read-only mode; contract tests prove the defaults are `sign=True` and read-only results carry a machine-readable unsigned reason.
- [ ] BLOCK, INDETERMINATE, degraded, exempt, and unattested local-source results remain unsigned; table-driven tests verify each guard.
- [ ] A PASS with an attempted signature-persistence failure returns the established retryable nonzero disposition and identifies `sign-review` as the recovery path; `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py` verifies the behavior.
- [ ] Re-running the default operation with a current claim-valid attestation reuses it without an LLM call or duplicate signature event; an interface test verifies both conditions.
- [ ] `rebar review` plan help, `rebar review-plan --help`, and MCP tool documentation state that PASS signs by default and name the explicit unsigned/read-only exceptions; help/reference drift tests verify the generated text.
- [ ] `docs/cli-reference.md`, `docs/user-guide.md`, `docs/llm-framework.md`, `docs/plan-review-gate.md`, and `docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md` accurately describe sign-on-PASS, explicit opt-out, never-sign outcomes, and `sign-review` recovery; documentation checks pass.
- [ ] `make lint`, `make typecheck`, and the default non-integration pytest suite exit zero.
data.title: Plan review PASS does not sign an attestation by default on every public route
